suggestions.title: Whiteboard text is invisible in dark mode â€” duplicate `color` property
suggestions.description: Office.css lines 1113 and 1122: `.office-whiteboard` sets `color: #c4b5fd` (light lavender) and then immediately overrides it with `color: #333` (dark grey) in the SAME rule. The computed text color in dark mode is therefore #333 on a #14122a background â€” essentially invisible. The emoji in strings like 'đźŹ† SHIP IT' may still render, but the text part won't. Worst case: 'TODO: ???' (slow-day vibe, no emoji) is completely invisible. Fix: remove the `color: #333` at line 1122 from the base rule; it belongs only in the `[data-theme='light']` override already present at line 1133.

suggestions.title: `stat-card--stuck` click target is keyboard-inaccessible
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 426â€“439: the 'Stuck workers' stat card uses a plain `<div onClick={...}>` with no `tabIndex`, `role`, `aria-label`, or `onKeyDown` handler. Keyboard-only users cannot trigger the scroll-to-stuck-worker action. Fix: add `tabIndex={0}`, `role='button'`, `aria-label='Scroll to stuck worker: {name}'`, and `onKeyDown={(e) => { if (e.key === 'Enter' || e.key === ' ') { e.preventDefault(); ...scroll } }}`.

suggestions.title: Arrow-key tab navigation does not update `window.location.hash`
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 631â€“640: `handleTabKeyDown` calls `setTab(...)` on ArrowLeft/ArrowRight but never updates `window.location.hash`. The two `onClick` handlers (lines 1013, 1026) correctly call `window.location.hash = 'office'/'dashboard'`. So clicking a tab updates the URL; pressing arrow keys does not. Hash-based deep links and browser back/forward break when the keyboard is used to switch tabs. Fix: add `window.location.hash = TABS[(newIdx)]` inside both arrow key branches of `handleTabKeyDown`.

suggestions.title: `stuckWorkers[0].emoji` renders literal 'undefined' string
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 436: `\`${stuckWorkers[0].emoji} ${stuckWorkers[0].name}\`` â€” if the stuck worker has no `emoji` field (it's optional and not present in DEFAULT_ROSTER entries except Luna), the template literal renders the string 'undefined' before the name. Example output: 'undefined Carlos Â· Working for 3h'. Fix: use `${stuckWorkers[0].emoji ?? ''} ${stuckWorkers[0].name}`.trim() or use a separate expression.

suggestions.title: `activityLogLenRef` is set but never read â€” dead ref
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 745: `const activityLogLenRef = useRef(0)` and line 747: `useEffect(() => { activityLogLenRef.current = activityLog.length }, [...])`. This ref is assigned on every activity update but is never read anywhere in the component. Likely a leftover from a dropped browser-notification feature (which would explain why Review #17 flagged 'No background tab notification on worker events' as still missing). Remove the ref and its effect unless the notification feature is being actively built.

suggestions.title: `stableActivityLog` is a misleading no-op memoization
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 746: `const stableActivityLog = useMemo(() => activityLog, [activityLog])`. This returns the exact same array reference as `activityLog` â€” the useMemo wrapper adds zero stabilization since it re-runs every time `activityLog` changes anyway. The name implies a stable reference but provides none. Either remove it and use `activityLog` directly, or if the intent was to prevent unnecessary re-renders in WorkerCard, a real selector/comparison function or a separate memoized slice would be needed.

suggestions.title: `ProgressBar` receives `updatedAt` prop that is never used
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 166: `function ProgressBar({ progress, updatedAt, startedAt })` â€” `updatedAt` is destructured but is not referenced anywhere in the component body. Only `startedAt` is used for ETA calculation. The prop is passed at line 303 (`updatedAt={worker.updated_at}`), creating a false impression that it affects output. Remove the prop from the signature and the call site.

suggestions.title: Dead CSS classes in mobile media query â€” stale from old refactor
suggestions.description:
App.css lines 1065â€“1082 (`@media (max-width: 480px)` block from 'Luna Review #41'): references three class names that do not exist anywhere in the JSX. `.tab-content` (doesn't exist; content is in `role=tabpanel` divs), `.header-subtitle` (doesn't exist; the subtitle is a `

` inside `.header-title`), and `.header h1` (the h1 lives inside `.header-title`, not directly in `.header`). These rules never fire. Clean them up to reduce confusion.

suggestions.title: Excessive concurrent CSS animations â€” still unaddressed from review #17
suggestions.description: Counting active infinite animations in a typical 'crushing' state with 3 working + 2 idle workers: manager-wobble, enter-officeĂ—3, bobĂ—3, deep-glow+deep-focus-halo (if >45min), 2Ă—wander pairs, dust-drift, 4Ă—star-twinkle (midnight), 2Ă—cloud-drift, 12Ă—ambient-drift (app-level) + 7Ă—ambient-drift (office-level) = 19 ambient particles, desk-pulseĂ—3, lamp-pulseĂ—3, typing-bounceĂ—9 (3 dots Ă— 3 chars), coffee-steamĂ—3, plant-swayĂ—3, heat-shimmer (on-fire), full-sync-pulse, vibe-crush (monitor). On a low-end phone this is 50+ simultaneous `animation` properties with `infinite` duration. No `will-change` is set, no animation budget management, and the reduced-motion block only helps users with that preference. Consider throttling or removing animations when the tab is hidden, or limiting concurrent wander chars.

suggestions.title: Focus ring applied to inner `.char__avatar`, not the focusable `.char` element
suggestions.description: Office.css line 683: `.char:focus-visible .char__avatar { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 2px; }`. The focusable element with `tabIndex={0}` is `.char`, not its child `.char__avatar`. Applying the visual ring to the inner element means it clips under the avatar border, may be partially hidden, and is offset from the actual focused element boundary. A keyboard user tabbing through workers will see an outline that doesn't correctly frame the interactive region. Fix: move the focus-visible style to `.char:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent); outline-offset: 3px; }` directly.

suggestions.title: SVG gradient ID `luna-hair-luna` is document-scoped â€” clashes when rendered twice
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 252: `const gradId = \`luna-hair-${workerId}\`` always resolves to `luna-hair-luna` for Luna. SVG `<linearGradient id>` values are document-scoped in SVG 1.1. When Luna appears in both the office floor (as a ghost or working character) and in the dashboard's WorkerCard avatar at the same time, two `<linearGradient id='luna-hair-luna'>` elements exist in the DOM. The second one silently wins, and the reference in the first SVG may point to wrong colors. Fix: include a render-time unique suffix (e.g., using a `useId()` hook or a counter) or scope the gradient to a `<defs>` inside the exact SVG element using a `#fragment` reference only within that SVG.

suggestions.title: `.live-badge` in `prefers-reduced-motion` block targets nonexistent class â€” still from review #17
suggestions.description: App.css line 897: the reduced-motion block lists `.live-badge` in its animation reset selector. This class does not exist anywhere â€” the live indicator uses `.live-dot.live`. The class was presumably renamed at some point. It's harmless but contributes to selector noise and misleads anyone debugging animation suppression.

suggestions.title: Double confetti layers can fire simultaneously â€” 60 animated spans at once
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 914â€“930: `confettiActive` (vibe rank increase) and `workerConfetti` (worker task completion) are independent states, both rendering 30 `<span>` elements inside `.confetti-layer`. They can both be truthy at the same time (e.g., a worker completes a task AND vibe rank increases in the same poll cycle). This spawns 60 simultaneously animated confetti elements. Not a crash, but a performance spike on an already animation-heavy page. Fix: merge into a single confetti state with priority (worker confetti > vibe confetti), or render only one layer at a time.

suggestions.title: Activity log still has no local persistence â€” third time reporting this
suggestions.description: Activity log is fetched fresh each poll cycle and capped to the last 20 items from the API response. If the user closes and reopens the tab, all activity history is gone. The `VIBE_HISTORY_KEY` mechanism already uses localStorage for vibe history (App.jsx line 843) â€” the same pattern could apply to activity log. Consider caching the last N activity entries in localStorage under a `bloberto-activity-log` key and merging on load to give continuity across sessions.
